title: Learning Programming with Generative AI: A Pre-University Pilot Study
data:
ST Yong, LY Chin, R Matthews, B Ramayah- 2026 14th International Conference on, 2026
Generative AI (GenAI) is redefining how programming is taught and learned. GenAI 
can perform various programming tasks such as coding and debugging. This 
research examines students' experiences with ChatGPT-4 and Microsoft Copilot in 
programming education. A mixed-methods approach was employed to collect both 
quantitative and qualitative data through programming exercises and interviews. The 
study involved 148 pre-university students who used both GenAI tools to complete
Trch dn: Refining chatgpt-generated code: Characterizing and mitigating

title: Optimizing and fortifying AI software through the lens of artifact synthesis
data:
J SHI - 2026
Artificial Intelligence (AI) has transformed the software landscape, ushering in a new 
era of intelligent systems that increasingly shape our daily lives. This transformation 
is evident in various domains, including Software Engineering (SE), where Large 
Language Models (LLMs) support many development tools, and control systems, 
where self-driving cars and autonomous drones rely on deep learning models for 
real-time decision-making. These AI systems are collectively referred to as AI
